title: File upload component with drag-and-drop support
purpose: Provides a reusable file upload UI component with validation, drag-and-drop functionality, and file size limits.
module_context: Exports a React functional component FileUpload that accepts various props for customization and handles file selection, validation, and display of selected files.

steps.content: Check if a maximum file size limit is defined.
For each file in the list, verify that its size does not exceed the maximum limit.
IF any file exceeds the limit, return an error message indicating the oversized file.
ELSE return a valid status with no error.
If no files are provided, return immediately.
Validate the provided files against size limits.
IF validation fails, set the error state and return.
ELSE clear any previous error state.
Update the selected files state with the validated files.
Notify the parent component of the file selection via the onChange callback.
Prevent the default drag-and-drop behavior.
Stop event propagation to prevent parent handlers from firing.
Reset the drag active state to false.
IF the component is disabled, return without processing files.
Process the dropped files through the standard file handling logic.
Prevent the default drag-and-drop behavior.
Stop event propagation to prevent parent handlers from firing.
IF the component is not disabled, set the drag active state to true.
Prevent the default drag-and-drop behavior.
Stop event propagation to prevent parent handlers from firing.
Reset the drag active state to false.
Extract the files from the input change event.
Process the selected files through the standard file handling logic.
IF the component is not disabled, programmatically trigger a click on the hidden file input.
Convert bytes to a human-readable format with appropriate units.
IF bytes are less than 1024, return the value in bytes.
ELSE IF bytes are less than 1024 kilobytes, return the value in kilobytes.
ELSE return the value in megabytes.
